name: Mercedes-Benz
philosophy: An inspired interpretation of Mercedes-Benz — the Stuttgart luxury automotive house whose brand language has been the engineering-to-art template for a century. Canvas is graphite-cool dark (#1a1d24) on flagship product pages, or warm cream-white on heritage and editorial pages. Type runs Corporate A — the proprietary Mercedes grotesk — at every text tier, paired with the iconic three-pointed-star mark. The signature is the three-pointed star itself, treated with absolute discipline: chrome silver on dark backgrounds, ink on light. Photography is studio-grade luxury: S-Class sedans photographed under dramatic side-light, EQ-line electric vehicles in architectural settings, AMG performance cars on race tracks. The voice is 'Das Beste oder nichts' — the best or nothing — translated as quiet engineering confidence, never bombast.

design_language.motion_signature: Cinematic luxury. Hero videos auto-play silent with slow tracking shots — S-Class sedans gliding past architecture, EQ-line vehicles in showroom-light. Cross-fades 1.5s. The three-pointed-star mark spins slowly (~6s loop) on configurator pages as a brand-affirmation motif. Hover transitions are subtle — opacity changes, never scale lift.

trademark_signals: Three-pointed star mark — the most-recognized automotive logo on the planet — chrome silver on dark, ink-on-light, never tinted, never restyled
Mercedes blue (#00adef) — the electric-line accent reserved for EQ (electric vehicle) sub-brand pages and EV configurator highlights — never as the primary CTA on AMG or S-Class pages
Dual canvas — graphite-cool dark for flagship product, warm cream-white for heritage and corporate editorial — the brand chooses canvas by content type, never mixes within a page
Cinematic studio photography — S-Class sedans under dramatic side-light, EQ vehicles in architectural settings, AMG cars on race tracks — never lifestyle staging with families
Corporate A grotesk at every tier — the proprietary face that reads as 'engineering institution', refusing the serif-luxury convention of Rolls-Royce or Bentley
ai_slop_avoided: Don't tint the three-pointed star. The mark is chrome silver on dark, ink on light — applying brand-blue or any chromatic fill to the star breaks a century of equity
Don't paint AMG pages in EQ blue. The Mercedes blue (#00adef) is reserved for EQ electric-vehicle sub-brand surfaces only
Don't substitute Corporate A with a fashion serif. The grotesk IS the engineering voice — serif reads as Rolls-Royce off-brand
Don't show vehicles with people inside. Mercedes hero photography is product-led, often with the cabin empty — refusing the family-lifestyle convention
Don't strip the chrome on the wordmark. The 'Mercedes-Benz' wordmark with the dotted-rule between the words is the recognized lockup
